title: Cryptographic Hashes, SHA-2, SHA-3, and Commitment Boundaries · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Hash Not Authentication
summary: A download service calls SHA-256 encryption, truncates digests without analysis, uses MD5 for adversarial integrity, and hashes ambiguous JSON. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the hash not authentication increment in hash-purpose and digest verification contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and eviden…
objectives: Require an authenticated trusted expected digest or MAC/signature when attackers can modify both data and digest.
Confine MD5 and SHA-1 to explicitly non-adversarial compatibility or migration checks and reject security claims based on them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Hash Not Authentication · Theory Crypto Go Hash Not Authentic…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 3108 during a replicated device restart. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a hash by purpose while rejecting weak algorithms, ambiguous bytes, and unauthenticated digest claims; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Require an authenticated trusted expected digest or MAC/signature when attackers can modify both data and digest.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Crypto Go Hash Not Authentication contract for the supplied data.
